patterns.detector: Population diversity drops below 30% while fitness has not improved for 3+ generations. The population is converging prematurely on a local optimum.
patterns.reframe: Inject random individuals, increase mutation rate, and prefer structural over point mutations to escape the local optimum. Consider re-injecting hall-of-fame individuals with high diversity.

patterns.detector: Best fitness has not improved for N generations (N >= 5). Point mutations are not finding better solutions.
patterns.reframe: Switch to structural mutations. Try fundamentally different approaches: different algorithms, data structures, or control flow. Point mutations are exhausted in this region.

patterns.detector: Crossover offspring consistently have lower fitness than both parents. The parents may be too different for meaningful recombination.
patterns.reframe: Reduce crossover rate or restrict crossover to parents with similar fitness levels. The population may have diverged into incompatible lineages.

patterns.detector: Most mutations (>70%) produce individuals with worse fitness than their parents. The mutations are too destructive.
patterns.reframe: Shift mutation weights toward point mutations and reduce structural mutation rate. The current code is close to a good solution and needs fine-tuning, not restructuring.

patterns.detector: Most mutations (<10% difference from parent fitness) don't meaningfully change fitness. The mutations are too timid to explore.
patterns.reframe: Increase structural mutation rate and add more aggressive prompting. The search space near the current solutions is flat and needs larger jumps to find improvement.

patterns.detector: Fitness is high but the evolved code uses hardcoded values, lookup tables, or special-cases specific test inputs rather than solving the general problem.
patterns.reframe: Add diversity to test cases, penalize code length (parsimony), and explicitly instruct mutations to avoid hardcoding. Consider adding generalization metrics to the fitness function.

patterns.detector: Code length grows across generations without corresponding fitness improvement. Individuals accumulate dead code or unnecessary complexity.
patterns.reframe: Apply parsimony pressure: penalize code length in fitness, or prefer shorter code at equal fitness. Explicitly prompt mutations to simplify and remove dead code.

patterns.detector: A beneficial trait (algorithm, technique) that appeared in earlier generations has disappeared from the current population. Good genes were lost through drift.
patterns.reframe: Increase elitism count to preserve more good individuals. Re-inject hall-of-fame individuals that had the lost trait. Consider increasing population size to maintain diversity.
